title: alisma plantago-aquatica subsp. latifolium
intro: Alisma plantago-aquatica subsp. latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved water plantain, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Alismataceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alisma plantago-aquatica subsp. latifolium include reducing swelling and alleviating symptoms of edema, as well as supporting kidney function and lowering blood pressure. Alisma plantago-aquatica subsp. latifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and other herbal remedies.
